examples: The validator shall check that smart contracts are working appropriately.
The assistant must provide appropriate search functions for various properties.
The system shall provide appropriate search tools for finding auto parts
The system shall support ship placement that is fair and balanced, ensuring players cannot gain an unfair advantage. Placement should feel intuitive and allow for strategic variety.

The reinforcement learning agent shall optimize delivery routes often to account for changing traffic conditions, ensuring that vehicles arrive in a timely manner. The system should also consider fuel costs when appropriate.
The engine must be able to parse footnotes to identify contingent liabilities and other risk factors. The output should be organized in a way that is useful for financial analysts.
It logs each occupant's room transitions with timestamps.
The system validates each trade against the registry before confirmation.
The system shall calculate total expenses by summing all line items within the selected date range.

When a researcher submits a query containing at least three domain-specific terms (from the ACM Computing Classification System), the algorithm shall retrieve a minimum of 20 papers that have been published in the last 5 years. The results must include at least two papers from distinct subfields as defined by the CCS hierarchy to ensure topical diversity.

When a call arrives, the system shall match the caller's phone number to the customer database. If a matching customer record exists, the agent screen shall display the customer's name, account status, and up to five most recent call notes; if no match exists, the agent shall be prompted to create a new customer record.
Program Administrators and Selected Members shall be able to add a new clinical lab section for an existing clinical class into the System.
The library should use appropriate memory during complex calculations.
Route optimization uses minimal computational resources.
The controller responds quickly to motion events.

The system should be optimized so that the compiler performs efficiently under most normal workloads without slowing down the user's computer. It should aim for a speedy turnaround time for typical microcontroller projects.

When the automated debugger is processing multiple memory leak requests, it should perform in a way that doesn't frustrate developers. The response time needs to be fast enough to maintain high productivity across the engineering team.
The wearable application shall process biometric data in real time without noticeable lag. It should also make efficient use of the device’s memory to ensure smooth operation throughout the day.
The platform shall support 500 concurrent active calls.
Memory footprint shall not exceed 4 GB per node for 1 TB of data.
CPU utilization shall not exceed 70% during normal operation.

The app shall synchronize credential status updates (revocation list) over a 4G connection with a payload size of up to 5 MB in under 10 seconds. The sync shall be performed in the background without affecting foreground presentation latency, and the sync process must not increase CPU utilization by more than 20% above idle.

All reporting endpoints shall return results within 5 seconds for date ranges covering up to 1 million leads. Queries spanning longer periods shall display a progress indicator and allow cancellation.
The prepaid balance inquiry service shall return a response within 200 milliseconds for 95% of requests. This limit applies even when the system is handling a baseline load of 500 simultaneous users.
It shares resources without causing conflicts.
The inventory system should work well with various external ERP platforms.
Platform shall work with common CRM systems.

The system must be able to import agent schedules from commonly used workforce management tools. The integration should be straightforward and support typical schedule formats without extensive custom development.

The suite should be compatible with most common operating systems used in the enterprise today. It must ensure that it doesn't cause any major issues when running alongside various third-party antivirus software.

The trading platform shall be compatible with major carbon registries to enable seamless settlement of trades. It should also share market data with third‑party analytics tools without causing conflicts.
The agent exchanges inventory data via REST API over HTTPS using JSON.
The platform shall exchange data with external systems using the SPARQL 1.1 Protocol.
The software shall operate on Chrome versions 120 and higher.

When the controller runs alongside a voice assistant like Alexa or Google Home, it must not interfere with their operation. Data exchanged with external weather services should be in a common format that those services typically accept.

The validator node shall support bi-directional data exchange with Hyperledger Fabric v2.5 through the Inter-Blockchain Communication (IBC) protocol. All exchanged packets must strictly follow the ICS-20 specification for cross-chain transfers

For integration with Google Analytics 4, the CMS shall embed the GA4 measurement ID in all published web pages and send enhanced ecommerce events for article reads, with the exact event parameters (article_id, title, author, publish_date) as defined in the integration guide version 1.2, validated by a conformance test suite.
The dashboard should be user-friendly
Users can learn the dashboard quickly.
The interface must look appropriate and provide helpful assistance when needed.

Users with varying levels of technical skill should be able to interpret the agent's suggested routing changes. The visualization of delivery routes must be clear enough for most stakeholders to understand.

The software should feature an inviting interface that motivates yard workers to complete their inventory tasks quickly. It should look professional while ensuring that important features are mostly obvious to the average user.

The interface needs to be inclusive and accessible for people with different backgrounds or physical capabilities. It should adapt appropriately to ensure that all navigation elements are intuitive enough for various skill levels.
After logging into the website, users shall be able to access their streaming movies within no more than 2 clicks.
Error messages display the invalid field and a suggested range.
The app requires exactly two taps to toggle a light.

New users shall be able to identify the 'Map Zoom' and 'Event Filter' functions within 10 seconds of first seeing the interface. This shall be verified by a usability test with 10 participants achieving a 100% success rate.

To protect against user errors, the system must prompt for a second confirmation before an agent can delete a client record. The prompt must require the user to select 'Confirm' from a two-option modal dialog to proceed.

A contextual help panel shall be available via a '?' icon in the top right corner. The panel shall display step-by-step instructions for the current screen and a glossary of lead scoring terms with definitions accessible in under two clicks.
The system tolerates most hardware failures.
System shall recover gracefully from crashes.
The website will be available to users 24 hours a day, every day of the year.
The system must be available for use between 7:00AM and 11:59PM all days of the year.

In the event of a server crash, the system should automatically recover and restore all league data without significant data loss. The recovery process must be fast enough that users do not notice prolonged interruptions.

The system should maintain data integrity even when faced with common software defects. It is expected that recovery procedures will bring the system back to a consistent state in a reasonable timeframe.
The agent restarts within 30 seconds of a crash.
The system shall maintain a monthly uptime of 99.9%.
The system shall mirror all card transactions to a secondary server.

When the PostgreSQL database connection fails, the RL agent shall retry the query up to 3 times with exponential backoff (initial delay 1 second, doubling each retry). If all retries fail, the agent must persist the pending route update to a local SQLite file and resume normal operation within 5 seconds, logging the failure with error code DB-504.

If the primary telephony gateway fails, the system shall automatically switch to a secondary gateway within 30 seconds. During failover, active calls shall remain connected, and new calls shall be routed without data loss. The switchover must be transparent to agents and customers.
After an unexpected restart, the system shall restore the most recent dashboard configuration and display a notification: 'Dashboard restored. Data may be up to 5 minutes old.
All actions are logged for accountability.
User actions within the framework should be logged often to ensure accountability
The system shall prevent unauthorized people from accessing the city utility grid models.

The system must verify that users are who they say they are before allowing them to access the reports. Access levels should be restricted appropriately so that only the right people can see private scan results.

To maintain integrity, the tool must check that the firmware hasn't been tampered with by hackers or other malicious actors during the upload process. The system should alert the user if something looks suspicious with the file transfer.
The system must resist common cyber attacks that might disrupt route optimization. If a security incident occurs, the agent should be able to continue operating in a degraded but functional mode.
All API calls require OAuth 2.0 with JWTs.
Access to delete listings shall require exactly two distinct authorization roles.
The tool must authenticate users via the enterprise LDAP server before granting access.

The PDA shall enforce a PIN policy requiring exactly 6 digits with no repeating characters. The system must lock the application after three consecutive failed attempts and require a server-side reset by an administrator.

Every job submission and data access event shall be logged with: user ID, timestamp, job ID, dataset identifiers, and a SHA‑256 checksum of the input configuration; logs shall be stored in a write‑only audit service with 1‑year retention and protected from modification via blockchain‑based integrity anchors (using Ethereum smart contract v2.1).

The application must log every credit card number access event, including the timestamp, the unique Employee ID, and the specific record modified. These logs must be stored in a read-only format for a period of exactly seven years.
The portal code should be easy to modify for future financial updates.
Modules are loosely coupled and reusable.
The warehousing module must be modular enough for future inventory changes.

The agent's routing logic shall be structured so that changes to the reward function do not require extensive modifications elsewhere. Developers should find it straightforward to add new supply chain constraints without breaking existing functionality.

The transaction management platform needs to have a clean architecture so that engineers can find things quickly. This will help them fix bugs in a timely manner whenever a problem occurs with the card system.
The codebase must be well-structured and documented to enable efficient debugging and feature additions. It should be possible for a new developer to become productive quickly.
The system shall log all runtime errors with stack traces.
All core service modules shall have unit test coverage of at least 85%.
Cyclomatic complexity per function shall not exceed 12.

The call routing engine shall be implemented as a separate microservice with its own API, database, and deployment pipeline. Changes to the agent UI or call recording service shall require no modifications to the routing engine, and vice versa.

The codebase shall maintain an average cyclomatic complexity below 10 per function and a maximum of 18 for any single function, as measured by SonarQube analysis on every pull request; violations shall block merge unless waived by two senior developers.

The pattern matching library must be compiled as a standalone C++ header-only library to facilitate reusability in other projects. All public API functions must be documented using Doxygen tags to ensure 100% automated documentation generation.
The system shall be portable to different operating systems as needed.
The system scales for many concurrent users.
The system should handle more users as the project grows.

The system needs to adapt well to different monitor resolutions and screen configurations found in various command centers. This ensures that the situational awareness remains helpful across a wide range of different operational environments.
The system must handle seasonal spikes in order volume gracefully, scaling up its computational resources as needed. It should also support a range of database backends without major code changes.

The smart contract framework ought to be flexible enough to adapt to changing blockchain requirements over time. It should support a wide range of hardware setups so that the transition between different network nodes is fairly smooth.
The product will be able to handle up to 2,500 simultaneous users. The system will work with 2500 users at a time.
The system shall scale horizontally to support 100,000 active nodes.
The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.

The simulator must be deployable as a set of OCI-compliant containers via Helm charts. Successful deployment on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform must be achieved using the same configuration files without manual modification.

The software must be successfully installable on Ubuntu 22.04 and RHEL 9.0 using an Ansible playbook. The automated installation script shall verify 100% of required libraries before proceeding with the node deployment.

The application shall be installable silently (no user prompts) via command‑line on Windows 11 (x64) and macOS 14 (Apple Silicon) using the flags `/S` for Windows and `--silent` for macOS, with installation logs written to a predefined location, and must fully uninstall leaving no registry keys or user data behind when the uninstaller is run.
The system shall handle failures without causing harm.
The system shall warn users before unsafe operations.
After 2 hours of continuous play, the game shall display a 'Take a break' warning and force a 30-second pause.

The system shall implement fail‑safe mechanisms to prevent runaway jobs from consuming all available resources, ensuring that other critical workflows can still execute. Warnings should be issued when resource usage approaches dangerous levels.
When the system detects a potential misassignment that could affect sales performance, it should alert the appropriate personnel. The alert should be delivered in a timely manner.

When the autonomous vehicle's control system detects an imminent collision with a pedestrian at a speed exceeding 15 km/h, it must activate an audible alert (≥85 dB) and a flashing visual warning on the instrument panel within 100 milliseconds. The system must continue to issue these warnings until the driver intervenes.
The tool should not cause any dangerous code execution during the analysis.
Fail-safe: stop all route updates if GPS signal lost.

When the prepaid card transaction software detects an attempt to exceed the remaining card balance by more than 10%, it will display a warning message and the transaction will be automatically blocked.

The platform shall execute a 'Safe Shutdown' sequence if the knowledge graph consistency check fails for three consecutive cycles. This sequence must bring the system to a fail-safe operational state, preventing any risk of data corruption, inconsistent queries, or unsafe system behavior.

The system will continuously monitor the node's power consumption and trigger an immediate shutdown if it exceeds 500 watts for more than 5 seconds to prevent electrical damage to the physical circuit.

The fail-safe module shall monitor the validator's memory consumption every 200ms. If consumption exceeds 90% of available RAM for 5 seconds, the system must revert to 'Emergency-Lite' mode by disabling non-essential logging.
